note: Raw reader fan-out claims: verbatim maker-attribution quotes copied from Memory Alpha character and species pages. Input to scripts/ds9-maker-adjudicate.mjs, which pins + verifies them. scope=character keys a character_page; scope=species keys a species (attaches to all characters of that species). Not verdicts.

claims.quote: Runepp was played by [[John Paul Lona]], a [[Trekkie|fan]] who won a guest spot on DS9 for designing the make-up of the Rasiinian species.
recollected Makeup Supervisor [[Michael Westmore]], "''we made a thin skin that went over one of our stock Ferengi heads
[[Thomas E. Surprenant]] was her makeup artist.
In [[DS9 Season 4]], the makeup for Tora Ziyal was worked on by regular DS9 makeup sculptor [[Dean Jones]].
Ziyal's relatively subtle Cardassian features were produced by the DS9 makeup department.
Stated [[Michael Westmore]], "''Both actors looked exactly alike in body shape, head size and facial features, so we were able to use a duplicate set of [makeup] appliances on them.''"
The makeup on Tracee Lee Cocco was applied by [[Thomas E. Surprenant]].
[[Michael Westmore]] designed the makeup for Cretak.
The script's spacefaring lifeform was designed by Senior Illustrator [[John Eaves]]
When creating Martok's facial features for {{e|The Way of the Warrior}}, Makeup Designer [[Michael Westmore]] went for a unique look.
[[File:Dave Quashnick applies makeup to JG Hertzler.jpg|thumb|left|J.G. Hertzler undergoes his transformation to Martok]]
Alexander's makeup in ''The Next Generation'' episode "Cost of Living" was applied by makeup artist [[Tania McComas]].
This "Westmore Alien" was portrayed by regular background actor [[Bill Blair]]
Worf's ridge patterns evolved due to experimentation by [[Michael Westmore]], mainly for aesthetics, but also to be more comfortable for the actor.
His makeup artist was [[Dean Jones]].
her makeup artist was [[Richard Snell]].
Initially, the DS9 writing staff simply didn't like [[Michael Westmore]]'s alterations to make the Odan headpiece look more feminine.
This alien was designed and created by [[Michael Westmore]].
His makeup artist was [[Mary Kay Morse]].
[[Hank Edds]] was his makeup artist.
Considering how he could give the Bajorans a subtle alien appearance, Michael Westmore thought about adding a prosthetic make-up appliance to either the forehead, the end of the nose, or the cheeks.
Designing the head of a Lurian began with a sketch which Makeup Supervisor [[Michael Westmore]] illustrated on the back of a [[script]], labeling the image simply "alien".
Ultimately, Morn's prosthetic head was sculpted by [[Vincent Niebla]],

Much of the workload which Makeup Supervisor [[Michael Westmore]] had to contend with during the [[DS9 Season 7|seventh and final season]] of DS9 consisted of manufacturing Vorta ears for the character of Weyoun
The facial appearance of the Ferengi was designed by TNG Senior Illustrator [[Andrew Probert]] and refined and produced by Makeup Supervisor [[Michael Westmore]].
The facial appearance of the Ferengi was designed by TNG Senior Illustrator [[Andrew Probert]] and refined and produced by Makeup Supervisor [[Michael Westmore]].
[[Michael Westmore]] found the physical design of a Lethean was "really interesting," owing to it looking unlike anything previously depicted on {{s|DS9}}.
in the words of Makeup Supervisor [[Michael Westmore]].
The alien's makeup was designed by [[Michael Westmore]]
Vulcan ear prosthetics, in encapsulated silicone, were created by makeup artist [[David Snyder]]

The Rasiinian in "The Muse" was played by [[John Paul Lona]]. He won a guest spot on DS9 for designing the makeup of the Rasiinian species in the [[Playmates Toys]]' "Design-an-Alien" contest.

explained Make-Up Supervisor [[Michael Westmore]].
when the wardrobe department first made Breen costumes, they were kind of simple; they just made a mask and put them on some guys who were going to stand in the background.
